news.title: Pune: Talathi & circle officer blocked mutation plea, lapses followed
news.para: A report by the Vikas Kharge-led panel revealed that revenue officials initially flagged irregularities in the Mundhwa land sale, blocking mutation. However, procedural lapses and failure to inform higher authorities weakened scrutiny. The tehsildar's neglect of duty and improper actions regarding the Botanical Survey of India's lease complicated the matter.

news.title: Property Share’s ₹245 crore SM REIT IPO to open on April 10
news.para: Property Share Investment Trust is launching its third SM REIT, PropShare Celestia. The initial public offering opens on April 10 and closes on April 16. It aims to raise ₹244.65 crore. Funds will acquire seven floors in Ahmedabad's Venus Stratum building. This commercial property is fully occupied by multinational and domestic companies.

news.title: PE inflows in Indian real estate rise 59% to $6.7 billion in 2025: Report
news.para: In 2025, the Indian real estate sector witnessed 59% increase in private equity investments, soaring to $6.7 billion. The office sector emerged as the frontrunner for these investments, closely followed by the booming data centres and residential markets.

news.title: Housing sales growth to moderate to 4-6% in FY27 amid high prices, steady demand: Crisil
news.para: Forecasts for FY27 indicate a notable slowdown in housing sales growth. The ongoing rise in property prices is influencing buyer interest, with sales likely to increase by a modest 4-6%. We may witness a rise in inventory levels, while price appreciation is expected to ease. The high-end housing sector seems set to keep demand alive.

news.title: Mumbai: 2,640 MHADA flats on offer, applications open today; draw on May 15
news.para: MHADA Mumbai Board is offering 2,640 apartments through a lottery. Applications open March 30, with the draw scheduled for May 15. Flats are available across various Mumbai locations for different income groups. The process involves online registration, form filling, and earnest money payment. Applicants need Aadhaar, PAN, domicile, and income proof.

news.title: Cement makers staring at ₹200 per tonne cost shock as West Asia war sends petcoke & packaging costs soaring
news.para: India's cement sector is experiencing a significant cost increase. Rising prices of petcoke, coal, and packaging materials are squeezing profit margins. Companies are attempting price hikes, but oversupply makes it difficult to pass on the full cost burden. Analysts suggest that improved demand and capacity utilization are key for future price stability.

news.title: Lulu Group arm buys Delhi's Sunder Nagar bungalow for ₹89.5 crore
news.para: Fair Exports (India) Pvt Ltd, a subsidiary of Lulu Group International, has acquired a bungalow in Delhi's Sunder Nagar for Rs 89.5 crore. The transaction, which saw the firm pay Rs 6.26 crore in stamp duty, highlights Sunder Nagar's growing appeal to high-net-worth individuals seeking prime residential properties in Lutyens' Delhi.

news.title: How PropTech and data strategy are redefining India’s real estate advisory in 2026
news.para: Indian real estate is embracing data-driven strategies. Advisors are becoming PropTech Strategists, focusing on digital appeal and micro-markets. Conversational commerce and collaborative marketing are key. Godrej Properties and Meta India are empowering advisors with advanced digital tools.

news.title: Golden Growth Fund acquires land parcel in South Delhi's Gulmohar Park
news.para: Golden Growth Fund has acquired land in South Delhi's Gulmohar Park. A new residential project will be developed on this plot. The project is expected to generate revenue of around ₹100 crore. Redevelopment is increasing in premium South Delhi colonies due to limited land and high demand for luxury floors.

news.title: Thane: 11 residents cheated with fake housing scheme; credentials misused for transactions
news.para: Fraudsters in Thane duped 11 residents by promising affordable housing under a government scheme. The accused collected Rs 5,000 each from victims, totaling Rs 55,000, and then forged their credentials to open bank accounts for larger financial transactions. Police have registered a case against three individuals for cheating and forgery.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad: Developers not supplying water to societies in PCMC areas
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad Municipal Corporation (PCMC) has warned developers of strict action for failing to provide water to housing societies as per written undertakings. Builders had promised to arrange water until civic infrastructure was operational, but many have not honored their commitments.

news.title: Haryana RERA orders refund with interest in BPTP's Park Terra case, rejects limitation plea
news.para: Haryana Real Estate Regulatory Authority has ordered BPTP Limited to refund Rs 18.12 lakh with 2% interest to a homebuyer. The developer's plea that the complaint was time-barred was rejected. The authority found the developer liable for a continuing breach. BPTP had cancelled the homebuyer's allotment due to non-payment.

news.title: Prayagraj development body plans project management unit for streamlined urban projects
news.para: The Prayagraj Development Authority (PDA) is establishing a Project Management Unit (PMU) to ensure integrated planning for residential schemes. This unit will incorporate essential infrastructure like sewer lines and roads from the initial planning stages, aiming to accelerate development and minimize technical lapses.

news.title: Office leasing rises 15% to 18.3 million sq ft across seven cities in Q1 2026: Colliers
news.para: Office leasing in India's top cities will see a 15% rise to 18.3 million sq ft in Q1 2026. Technology, BFSI, and global capability centers are driving this growth. Bengaluru and Hyderabad will lead leasing activity. Flexible workspace demand is also increasing significantly. New office supply remains robust, and vacancy levels are expected to decline, supporting rental increases.

news.title: Housing sales dip 7% quarter-on-quarter in Q1 2026 amid global uncertainty: Report
news.para: Housing sales in India's top cities saw a 7% dip in the first quarter of 2026. This sequential decline occurred despite a year-on-year increase in demand. Global uncertainties and rising costs impacted buyer sentiment. New launches, however, remained resilient, exceeding sales volumes. Unsold inventory levels also rose. Residential prices continued to firm up across major urban centers.

news.title: Lucknow civic body office hours extended till March 31 for tax deposit
news.para: Lucknow Municipal Corporation has extended working hours for its zonal offices, including Sunday and late into the night on March 30th and 31st, to boost commercial and house tax collection. This initiative aims to encourage taxpayers to clear dues before the financial year ends to avoid a 12% interest penalty on outstanding amounts.

news.title: Two construction firm directors in Mumbai held for duping NRI of ₹10 crore
news.para: Two directors of Ajanta Airawat Infrastructure LLP have been arrested. They are accused of cheating an NRI investor of Rs 10.5 crore. The investor had put in Rs 1.5 crore in 2012. Later, he extended a Rs 6 crore loan. A settlement agreed to repay Rs 10.5 crore with interest. The firm failed to pay or allot flats.

news.title: Banks can't claim priority over properties attached under PMLA: Bombay HC
news.para: The Bombay High Court ruled that the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) takes precedence over bank recovery laws like SARFAESI and RDB Act. This decision clarifies that banks cannot automatically claim priority over properties attached by the Enforcement Directorate as proceeds of crime, reinforcing the state's authority to confiscate illicit assets.

news.title: Inaugurated in haste? Wayanad township project slows to a crawl
news.para: Newly built houses for Wayanad landslide survivors remain unoccupied. Inaugurated nearly a month ago, pending plumbing and sewage works prevent families from moving in. The delay has ignited political debate, with the opposition accusing the government of an election stunt. The project faces worker shortages as migrant laborers return home for elections.

news.title: Hyderabad civic body locks over 100 big properties over tax dues
news.para: Hyderabad civic body is cracking down on major property tax defaulters, sealing over 100 commercial properties to recover hundreds of crores. High-profile establishments like Katriya Hotel and Aurora College face significant liabilities. The drive now includes vacant lands, with warnings of property auctions for non-payment. A One-Time Settlement scheme offers a 90% interest waiver.
